title: Repair RepoGround runtime false-green health and content-search contracts
goal: Fix the two runtime contract defects exposed by T025: the repository launcher probes /health although the API contract exposes /api/health, and retrieval artifacts populate path tokens while leaving FTS content empty so content queries silently return no hits.

acceptance.assertion: The canonical launcher and service contract use one tested health endpoint; a started service passes the launcher probe and an unavailable or wrong-version service fails closed.
A retrieval build from a fixture with source text writes non-empty searchable content rows, not only path_tokens, and preserves deterministic metadata.
A query for a symbol or phrase present only in file content returns the expected file; a path-only control remains functional and a missing phrase returns zero hits.
Regression tests fail against the pre-fix /health mismatch and empty-content artifact, and verify negative authentication, traversal and stale-index semantics.
Existing RepoGround CLI, MCP, release and legacy compatibility tests remain green; historical schemas and names are not rewritten.
The exact reviewed diff passes full local tests and GitHub CI, is merged, and is verified through a parallel runtime probe before production deployment.

rollback.trigger: The production service fails health or authentication checks, content rows become empty, path lookup regresses, or retrieval latency or artifact compatibility degrades materially.
rollback.procedure: Restore the saved pre-cutover unit from the T006 proof directory, point RepoGround back to retained runtime 9bca73f70021f941feb22883d0020f963530ff29, daemon-reload, restart repoground.service, and require health, version and path-query readback before further work.

conditions: a foreign RepoGround writer overlaps the intended files or worktree
the current main head differs from the recorded baseline before branch creation
the content source cannot be identified without changing public artifact semantics
the fix would require rewriting historical bundles or compatibility schemas

validated_acceptance.health-contract: PASS: canonical /api/health launcher contract, exact production version and unauthenticated 401 readback
validated_acceptance.content-projection: PASS: 2 of 2 fixture FTS rows contain searchable content in canary and production artifacts
validated_acceptance.content-query: PASS: content-only and path-only queries return src/pathonlymarker_t006.py; missing token returns zero
validated_acceptance.no-false-green: PASS: old runtime reproduced 0 content rows and zero content hits before the new runtime passed the same live fixture
validated_acceptance.compatibility: PASS: 4,385 tests passed with one skip and four known legacy warnings
validated_acceptance.published-proof: PASS: merged source fix, immutable current-main release, parallel canary, practical rollback, production cutover and final cleanup are hash-bound

does_not_establish: that every pre-existing retrieval bundle has been rebuilt
that legacy aliases may be removed
future RepoGround runtime immutability after later deployments
metadata.former_block_reason: RepoGround PR #1041 is merged and all source gates passed, but the mandatory parallel runtime probe, controlled deployment and live readback cannot be executed in this session because the installed Grabowski app has no registered tool namespace.
metadata.former_next_action: With a registered Grabowski host connector, build an immutable runtime from merge commit 7777f6fd690e504e11e26fbaa4ab5c4d2fc4a637, probe it in parallel, verify /api/health and content-only retrieval, prove rollback, then deploy and close T006 only after live readback.
metadata.resolution_summary: RepoGround source fix PR #1041 was already merged, but production still ran the earlier 9bca73f7 runtime and reproduced empty FTS content. Current main 3b3d6f19 passed 4,385 tests, was deployed first as an immutable parallel canary, passed content/path/missing-query and rollback proofs, then replaced production on port 8787 with exact version and auth readback.
